title: How the Cole Disaster Drove the U.S. to Develop New Warship Defenses
summary: The Navy destroyers enforcing a blockade of Iranian ports carry weapons fielded after an American warship was attacked and nearly sunk more than 25 years ago.

title: Trump and Iran Face Off in Iran War Negotiations
summary: As the United States and Iran make a second attempt at a deal, their negotiating styles are on a collision course.

title: Oil Prices Recede and Stocks Gain as Investors Eye Peace Talks
summary: Oil prices pulled back and stocks inched higher as investors looked to the possibility of a second round of peace talks between the United States and Iran.

title: Labor Secretary Resigns Amid Scandals, and F.B.I. Director Sues Over Drinking Claims
summary: Plus, what to know about Apple’s next C.E.O.

title: Virginia Redistricting Referendum: What to Watch
summary: In the latest gerrymandering clash before the midterm elections, voters in Virginia will decide on Tuesday whether to approve a map that would give Democrats more House seats.

title: Virginia Gov. Abigail Spanberger Stressed Pragmatism, But Politics Hound Her
summary: Gov. Abigail Spanberger of Virginia has emphasized practical governance as her brand, but the political fight over redistricting has dominated her term so far.

title: Democrat Betty Yee Leaves the California Governor’s Race
summary: Xavier Becerra, who once struggled to gain traction, has found growing support after Eric Swalwell left the race amid sexual harassment allegations.

title: Supreme Court Reviews F.C.C.’s Enforcement Power Against Communications Companies
summary: AT&T and Verizon were penalized millions of dollars for what the agency said was a failure to protect consumer information. The companies say they were deprived of their right to a jury trial.

title: Lori Chavez-DeRemer, Trump’s Labor Secretary, Steps Down Amid Internal Investigation
summary: Lori Chavez-DeRemer was facing multiple scandals and an internal inquiry into charges of misconduct.

title: A Wish List for the Man Replacing Tim Cook as Apple’s C.E.O.
summary: John Ternus, an Apple veteran who runs hardware engineering, will take over an extraordinarily profitable company in need of new ideas.

title: How Apple Became a $4 Trillion Company Under Tim Cook
summary: If the Steve Jobs era was defined by technological innovation, the Tim Cook period was one of exceptional financial growth.

title: What to Expect in Apple’s New Era
summary: Tim Cook’s replacement as chief executive, John Ternus, knows hardware engineering. Can he be a product visionary and a global diplomat?

title: Even Without Internet Access, Prisoners Are Trying to Benefit From A.I.
summary: Most prisons forbid online access. But inmates are finding ways to ask chatbots questions anyway.

title: Want to Speak to the Manager? At a New San Francisco Store, That’s A.I.
summary: Andon Market in San Francisco is billed as the first retail boutique run by an artificial intelligence agent. So far, the inventory seems random, and there are too many candles.

title: Japan to Sell More Weapons Abroad, Breaking With Postwar Pacifism
summary: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi reversed limits on arms exports as Japan faces rising threats from China and unpredictability from its main ally, the United States.

title: Louisiana Killer’s Threats to Himself and Others Hinted at Future Violence
summary: Shamar Elkins had been suffering severe mental health problems, his family said. His deadly rampage, which killed eight children, has profoundly scarred a community facing rising domestic violence.

title: What’s in a Name? For These Snails, Legal Protection
summary: Scientists are debating the classification of threatened mollusks that an Indigenous community relies on for their way of life.

title: ‘Wagyu’ Used to Guarantee Quality Beef. What Are You Paying for Today?
summary: Behind the scenes, competing forces battle for the reputation of prestige meat.

title: My Child Died of Measles
summary: If more people had gotten the vaccine, she might still be here.

title: Can Kevin Warsh Pass the Jerome Powell Test?
summary: The question of the central bank’s independence looms over the economy.

title: Pain at the Pump
summary: We look at the changes in gas prices.

title: Jimmy Kimmel Refers to Kash Patel’s Job in the Past Tense
summary: The late night host called him “our soon-to-be former F.B.I. director” after publication of an article in The Atlantic alleging excessive drinking and unexplained absences.

title: London Faces Disruption From Tube Drivers’ Strike
summary: The dispute over working conditions and hours was expected to cause widespread delays and service outages.

title: Canadian Tourist Is Killed in Shooting at Mexico’s Pyramids
summary: A gunman opened fire at the Teotihuacán archaeological site outside Mexico City before killing himself, the authorities said. Six Americans were among those injured, including two from gunshots.

title: Harvey Weinstein’s Third Trial on Rape Charge Opens in Manhattan
summary: Harvey Weinstein is accused of raping an actress in a Manhattan hotel more than a decade ago. After an overturned conviction and a hung jury, he will face the charge once again.

title: Fired U.K. Official Describes ‘Pressure’ From Starmer’s Office for Envoy’s Appointment
summary: The prime minister’s office was “dismissive” of security concerns about Peter Mandelson, a Jeffrey Epstein associate who became ambassador to Washington, the official said.
